principle: ONE canonical command surface for the whole fleet. Every legacy audit/report command, slash-command, and registry entry maps to exactly one of these or is retired. fabric-os orchestrates; bridge-os MPR engine scores; canon-os holds coverage. No parallel command vocabularies.

canonicalCommands.aaas:audit:
node platform/scripts/aaas-audit.mjs --pillar

[--micro ] [--tier foundational|transformational] [--all] — run pillar/micro audits (delegates to the bridge-os MPR engine)
canonicalCommands.aaas:report: node platform/scripts/aaas-report.mjs <umbrella>  — foundational-readiness | transformational-readiness | mpr-scorecard | remediation-roadmap

engine.note: The single scoring engine aaas:audit delegates to. Five-core runner (audit:five-core:run) is RETIRED — superseded by the 11-pillar MPR.

3-pnpm-scripts.action: retire legacy audit/* scripts; keep aaas:* + audit:mpr:repo:run

4-slash-commands.action: consolidate ~21 audit-*.md to the canonical surface (or retire)

5-agent-llm-files.action: remove legacy audit command refs; point to aaas:* surface

scrubProgress.1-files: ready — aaas:scrub built, dry-run 571 files; gated on front 2 (else regenerates)
scrubProgress.2-registries: handed off — XR-AUDIT-COMMAND-RECONCILE-001 to canon-os+bridge-os (P24, awaiting ack)
scrubProgress.3-pnpm-scripts: pending — retire legacy audit/* scripts fleet-wide
scrubProgress.4-slash-commands: DONE — 37 legacy audit commands archived to ~/.claude/commands-archive/legacy-audit-scrub-2026-06-28/; canonical aaas-audit + aaas-report installed
